mission.title: envoy: Support generic outbound proxy
mission.description: Support generic outbound proxy. Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
When using Envoy as a general egress proxy, there doesn't seem to be a way to have `http_connection_manager` routes send to a cluster and have the `:authority` header be used to select the destination.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support generic outbound proxy
steps.description:
This is the first step to upstream Istio JWT auth filter to Envoy.

Istio JWT auth filter is here [https://github.com/istio/proxy/tree/master/src/envoy/http/jwt_auth]

The issue to upstream to envoy is:  https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/2514

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support generic outbound proxy" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is the first step to upstream Istio JWT auth filter to Envoy.

Istio JWT auth filter is here [https://github.com/istio/proxy/tree/master/src/envoy/http/jwt_auth]

The issue to upstream to envoy is:  https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/2514

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
